free_text: Nuclear Charge Dispersion in Mass Chains 130-135 from
The Fission of U-238 by Medium-Energy Protons.
Subentry O0575025 has been corrected
according to O.Schwerer's remarks.
Last checking has been done.
The Uranium Target Foils, 130 mg/cm**2 Thickness, were
Used.
.The Two-Step Serber-Model.
.A 30 cm**3 Ge(Li) Detector.
In Experiments Involving Chemical Separation
Of Radioactive Tellurium from Other Fission Products,
134-Te was Used As the Internal Monitor to Eliminate
Chemical Yield Determination For this Element.
Bombardments Varied from 1 Min. To 20 Min. And
The Beam Currents from O.5 to 1 Mua.

Was Obtained by Considering Errors For the Monitor
Cross-Sections, Chemical Yields, Counting Rates,
Branching Ratios, Half-Lives and Detector Efficiency.

The Formation Cross-Section of the Xe-Isotopes were
Interpolated from Plots of the Formation Cross Sections
Of the I and Cs Isotopes Vs the N/Z Parameter.The Xe-
Cross Sections were Later Used to Aid Construction of
Isobaric Charge-Dispersion Curves.

.Uncertainties For Each Total Chain Yield have
Been Derived by a Computer-Fitting Procedure Which Sets
Up Charge Distribution Curves from Values at the Limits
Of Errors in the Yield of Each Chain Member.
